# UC-17.1.78 · ISE PSN Authentication Distribution Imbalance and Sticky-Session Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** ITOps &middot; **Type:** Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE has multiple servers. They should share work equally. We notice when one server suddenly does much more (or less) than the others, which usually means something is misconfigured.*

---

## Description

Detects when one PSN is receiving disproportionately more or fewer auths than its peers — usually caused by NAD misconfiguration (forgotten secondary RADIUS), load-balancer health-check failure, or sticky-session affinity gone wrong.

## Value

Imbalanced PSN load means one PSN works hard while others sit idle — and is often the first sign of a load-balancer or RADIUS-server-list misconfiguration. Catching it preserves capacity headroom on the busy PSN.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable secondary RADIUS server on a NAD; one PSN receives all auth traffic. UC-17.1.78 fires.

### Negative scenario

Balanced cluster. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5200
| stats count as auth_count by host
| eventstats avg(auth_count) as avg_count, stdev(auth_count) as stdev_count
| eval z_score=(auth_count - avg_count) / stdev_count
| where abs(z_score) > 2
```

## Visualization

Per-PSN auth distribution; z-score chart; load-balancer health.

## Known False Positives

- Geographic load distribution — campus PSNs serve only local NADs.
- Active/standby designs.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Load Distribution](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_install_ise.html)
